item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 19)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: U.S. concludes 8th night of strikes on Iran after Iranian attack kills 2 U.S. soldiers
item.description: In addition to the two U.S. service members killed, a third is missing in action, U.S. Central Command said.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Where's Maya Millete? Family, friends continue search for missing California mom
item.description: A California mother disappears without a trace – did her husband try to have a hex put on her so she wouldn't leave him?

item.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested by federal authorities Saturday in Miami.

item.title: 2 U.S. service members killed, 1 missing following Iranian strike on Jordan
item.description: U.S. forces launched a new round of airstrikes against Iran Saturday evening at the direction of President Trump following the deaths of two U.S. service members.

item.title: Colorado ICE center employee arrested after allegedly shooting protester
item.description: An employee of a group that operates an ICE detainment center in Aurora, Colorado, was arrested after police said he shot and wounded a woman who was taking part in a protest.

item.title: Healthcare costs associated with aging out of reach for many Americans
item.description: One New York couple depleted their retirement and savings accounts, paying out-of-pocket for healthcare, before they were able to access Medicaid funds.

item.title: Fans flock to see much-anticipated epic "The Odyssey"
item.description: For the first time in what seems like forever, there has been a rush to see a new blockbuster on a really big screen, as Christopher Nolan's epic "The Odyssey" arrived in theaters this weekend. Noel Brennan has more.

item.title: Northeast, Mid-Atlantic under tornadoes and flood risk as wildfire smoke lingers
item.description: A severe weather threat is in effect from the Ohio Valley through the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Wildfire smoke fouls air across Northeast
item.description: Smoke from hundreds of wildfires burning across Canada and Minnesota is creating air quality issues in the Northeast. There are also severe weather threats to the Midwest, as well as the risk of flash flooding in some areas. Meteorologist Andrew Kozak has the forecast.

item.title: Mexican president to attend World Cup final at Trump's invitation amid trade tensions
item.description: It will be the first meeting between President Trump and Sheinbaum since December after months of verbal sparring. Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney is also expected to attend.

item.title: World Cup culminates in Spain-Argentina final
item.description: The World Cup kicked off with 48 teams. Now only two remain, soccer powerhouses Spain and Argentina, who will face off Sunday in the much-anticipated final. Nicole Valdes reports from MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, where the game will be played.

item.title: Men's mile record broken for first time since 1999
item.description: British runner Josh Kerr ran the mile in 3 minutes, 42.66 seconds on Saturday, breaking a record that has stood since 1999.

item.title: British runner sets new men's mile record
item.description: British runner Josh Kerr on Saturday ran the mile in 3 minutes, 42.66 seconds, breaking a record that had stood since 1999.

item.title: Body found at Boston home owned by husband of Rep. Ayanna Pressley
item.description: Boston police are investigating the discovery Saturday of a dead body that was found at a property that is owned by the husband of Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley.

item.title: Costs put healthcare out of reach for many aging Americans
item.description: One New York couple depleted their retirement and savings accounts, paying out of pocket for healthcare before they were able to access Medicaid funds. They are among many Americans facing the same challenges. Jericka Duncan has the story.

item.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak sparks race to remove contaminated lettuce
item.description: A cyclospora outbreak, which has sickened thousands of people nationwide, is believed to have been traced to contaminated lettuce from Mexico supplied by produce giant Taylor Farms. Ian Lee has the latest.

item.title: Trump threatens new tariffs on Canada over wildfire smoke
item.description: Canada is engulfed by more than 900 wildfires that have sent smoke into the U.S., blanketing the Midwest and Northeast and causing air quality concerns. President Trump this week threatened to impose tougher tariffs on Canada because of the smoke. But experts say a fix to the wildfires isn't simple. Taurean Small has more.

item.title: 2 U.S. soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan military base
item.description: Two U.S. soldiers were killed and a third is missing following an Iranian strike on a military base in Jordan overnight Friday. Four other service members were wounded. At least 16 U.S. soldiers have died since the Iran war started in February. Anna Coren reports.

item.title: England holds on to defeat France 6-4 in wild World Cup third place match
item.description: It was the highest-scoring World Cup game since Hungary beat El Salvador 10-1 in 1982.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: 7/18: CBS Weekend News
item.description: At least two U.S. soldiers were killed in an Iranian attack on a military base in Jordan; Smoke from Canadian wildfires engulfs Northeast.

item.title: Target recalls over 200,000 children's sandals due to choking risk
item.description: Target said it received 23 reports of the sandal's pearls falling off the shoe.

item.title: Can you spot an AI image? Quiz shows how difficult identifying deepfakes has become.
item.description: An AI expert said the telltale signs people once relied on, such as distorted fingers, unnatural eyes, or other obvious visual flaws, have largely disappeared as technology has improved.

item.title: Teleprompter operator made over $100K betting on Trump speeches
item.description: After detecting suspicious activity on the teleprompter operator's account, Kalshi investigated and then referred the case to federal regulators.

item.title: Nearly 736,000 Pillsbury rolls recalled over possible glass contamination
item.description: The recall includes cases of Pillsbury "Hard Roll Dough" and "Kaiser Roll Dough" bread rolls, which are marketed to businesses.

item.title: "Take Me Home, Country Roads" and the universal pull towards home
item.description: "Take Me Home, Country Roads," a song about a longing for home (co-written by a songwriter who had never even been in West Virginia), has been embraced by the Mountain State in a big way, and has since been appropriated by singers around the world looking for their very own "place I belong." Correspondent Conor Knighton looks into the genesis and global impact of John Denver's first big hit; and with country star Brad Paisley about the special pull the song has for him. (Originally broadcast Dec. 26, 2021.)

item.title: Second Dan Sullivan can appear on Alaska Senate primary ballot, court rules
item.description: The Alaska Supreme Court ruled Monday that a man with the same name and party as Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan can challenge the sitting lawmaker in the state's Senate primary in August.
